source: Live Redis (production Upstash, unprefixed keys) over the FULL scorable universe (listScorableCountries(), 196 countries). Each country was scored twice with the real production scorer path (scoreAllDimensions -> buildDimensionList -> buildDomainList -> buildPillarList -> penalizedPillarScore, alpha=0.5) under live production flags (RESILIENCE_ENERGY_V2_ENABLED=true, RESILIENCE_PILLAR_COMBINE_ENABLED=true, RESILIENCE_SCHEMA_V2_ENABLED=true). Run A read the live WB-composite low-carbon payload; Run B overrode ONLY resilience:low-carbon-generation:v1 with the freshly-parsed OWID share-electricity-low-carbon payload. Every other input was held identical from live Redis, so the delta is purely attributable to the source swap.

decisionContext.question: Does migrating lowCarbonGenerationShare from the WB NUCL+RNEW+HYRO composite (live, frozen at 2021) to OWID share-electricity-low-carbon (~2025) keep the overall resilience score within the published acceptance gates?
decisionContext.oldSource: World Bank EG.ELC.NUCL.ZS + EG.ELC.RNEW.ZS + EG.ELC.HYRO.ZS, latest common component year per country (live max year 2021; 208 countries).
decisionContext.newSource: OWID Grapher share-electricity-low-carbon (Ember / Energy Institute), pre-aggregated renewables+nuclear share (live max year 2025; 209 countries mapped to ISO2).
decisionContext.acceptanceGates: Spearman vs baseline >= 0.85; no country moves > 15 points overall (methodology v2.1 plan section 6).

findings.headline: The source swap is well inside every acceptance gate. Overall rank order is almost perfectly preserved (Spearman 0.99758 across 196 countries) and the largest single-country overall move is 4.04 points (Taiwan); zero countries move more than 5 points. The swap ALSO corrects implausible WB-composite values: the WB feed reported many developing-country grids at ~75-100% low-carbon (Mali 100, Cambodia 100, Honduras 98.5) where OWID reports 19-55%. The large energy-DIMENSION moves are concentrated on exactly those over-counted countries, i.e. the migration fixes a data-quality defect in addition to refreshing the cadence.

rankPreservation.interpretation: 0.99758 is far above the >= 0.85 gate; methodology changes in published composite indicators typically target Spearman >= 0.90 to be considered rank-stable.

overallScoreImpact.interpretation: Pillar/domain weighting plus the min-pillar penalty damp the 0.20-weight energy-dimension change down to a handful of <5-point overall moves. No country approaches the 15-point gate.

energyDimensionImpact.interpretation: Dimension-level moves are larger (expected, since the change lands directly on a 0.20-weight indicator) but overwhelmingly downward for countries the WB composite had over-counted. Taiwan (energy 77->43) and Mali (94->72) are the largest; both reflect more credible OWID low-carbon shares.

coverageDelta.note: OWID is NOT a strict superset of the retired WB feed: 5 micro-states (Andorra + 4 Pacific micro-states) drop out and 2 (Taiwan, Macau) are gained. The 5 dropped micro-states see their energy dimension rise ~20 points (overall +1.4 to +1.8) because the low-carbon indicator's 0.20 weight redistributes when null. This coverage churn motivated lowering the seeder's MIN_COUNTRIES validation floor from 200 to 180 (a 200 floor left only a ~9-country margin against false STALE_SEED).

verdict: PASS — both acceptance gates clear with large margin (Spearman 0.99758 >> 0.85; max overall move 4.04 << 15). Safe to migrate. The swap additionally corrects WB low-carbon over-counting for developing-country grids.
